check.command: python -m pytest -q && python -m ruff check . && python -m basedpyright && python -m compileall -q orthoxrd tests app.py

frontend.command: cd frontend && bun test && bun run typecheck && bun run build

branchName.instructions: Use prefixes feat/, fix/, docs/, refactor/, test/, or chore/. Personal or exploratory branches use mb/. Examples: feat/export-excel-v2, fix/fit-weight-default, docs/contributing-checks, mb/explore-live-canvas. Prefer short kebab-case after the prefix. Do not use spaces.

commitMessage.instructions: Follow Conventional Commits with a scope: type(scope): summary. Types: feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore. Scopes often: fit, export, live, ui, powder, structure, batch, sweep. Keep the subject concise; body may note scientific impact on y, shuffle, intensity model, or export schema. Do not commit __pycache__, local exports, Playwright sessions, or QA screenshots.

pullRequest.instructions: Start with a short summary of what changed and why. Then include a Test plan section. Test plan must cover: python -m pytest -q; python -m ruff check .; and if frontend/Live Canvas changed: cd frontend && bun test && bun run typecheck && bun run build. For scientific changes, note which regression tests cover units, boundaries, normalisation, or physical invariants. Never describe calculated model peak intensity as experimental absolute intensity.
